note: Submodule of SearchStax. A one-off tool that migrates an existing (non-SearchStax) Solr Search API server's configuration into SearchStax Site Search, meant to be uninstalled afterwards. LIMITATION: every real action (migrate-server, copy-index, set-auth-token) drives the live SearchStax account API and needs a subscription + network access, so this submodule is documented with recipe-only evals (response-graded, no site changes and no medium/introspection or hard/execution tiers).

evals.prompt: Using the Solr to SearchStax Site Search Migration submodule on Drupal 11, which Drush command migrates an existing Solr Search API server to SearchStax, and how do you tell it which server to migrate and which SearchStax account/app to target?
evals.expected_output: drush searchstax:migrate-server <server_id> migrates the given Search API Solr server; pass --account, --app-id, optional --languages and --copy-from-view (VIEW_ID:DISPLAY_ID) to pick the SearchStax target and what to copy. Add --no-interaction to fail instead of prompting for missing values.

evals.prompt: The migration commands in the Solr to SearchStax submodule need a valid SearchStax auth token. Which Drush command sets one for headless use, and how do you control how long it stays valid?
evals.expected_output: drush searchstax:set-auth-token <token> stores a currently-valid SearchStax auth token (retrieved from the SearchStax obtain-auth-token API); use --expire=SECONDS to set its TTL, which defaults to 24 hours.

evals.prompt: Where is the admin UI for the Solr to SearchStax Site Search Migration submodule, which permission gates it, and what does the module tell you to do once the migration is finished?
evals.expected_output: The migration overview is at /admin/config/search/solr-to-searchstax-ss-migration (route solr_to_searchstax_ss_migration.overview), gated by the administer search_api permission. It lists migratable Solr servers; the module is intended to be uninstalled again once the migration is complete.
